pain_point_label: Actuals Reporting Lag & Real-Time Variance Blindness

matches.match_rationale: The 3-4 week close lag is partly caused by inconsistent transaction records needing manual normalization.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated ETL layer that ingests site-level ledger exports and normalizes them to a standard schema in near-real-time.

matches.match_rationale: AI summarization can auto-generate plain-language variance commentary from structured financial data.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A report generation layer that takes partially-closed actuals and produces a draft board pack narrative with flagged variances.

matches.match_rationale: Time series forecasting on high-frequency leading indicators can project month-end actuals mid-month.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A mid-month forecasting dashboard that projects site-level month-end spend by category.

matches.match_rationale: Transaction-level anomaly detection can surface unexpected spend patterns in near-real-time.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An always-on anomaly monitor that flags site-level transactions deviating from expected patterns within 48 hours of posting.

matches.match_rationale: The 3-4 week close lag is a workflow problem that a task automation agent can orchestrate.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous close orchestration agent that manages the month-end checklist across all sites.
